summary:
Vertical growth of the maxilla occurs through ongoing development at its sutural connections and not via alternative anatomical pathways mentioned elsewhere.
The primary mechanism for vertical expansion of the maxilla involves active growth along facial sutures without significant contribution from adjacent structures or joint types listed among options. To refine this summary further while adhering strictly within two concise statements focusing only on clinically pertinent aspects related directly back towards addressing how/why it specifically happens that way would be more precise yet still brief enough per instructions provided hereafter below:
